name: Wafer Bay
region: Central America, Costa Rica, Cocos Island
description: Dive into the pristine waters of Cocos Island, where Wafer Bay offers thrilling encounters with schooling hammerheads and massive manta rays. The steep volcanic walls are draped in vibrant corals, while strong currents attract pelagic action. Expect clear visibility and dramatic drop-offs teeming with white-tip reef sharks and turtles. This remote Pacific site is a haven for advanced adventurers seeking big animal encounters.

name: Punta Maria
region: Central America, Costa Rica, Cocos Island
description: Off the coast of Cocos Island, this site offers thrilling drift dives along steep volcanic walls teeming with marine life. Strong currents attract large pelagics like hammerheads, rays, and schooling fish. The dramatic underwater topography features caves and overhangs, often visited by white-tip reef sharks. Visibility is excellent, typically over 30 meters. Suitable for experienced divers due to challenging conditions.

name: Bajo Manuelita
region: Central America, Costa Rica, Cocos Island
description: A submerged pinnacle off Cocos Island teems with marine life. Schools of hammerhead sharks patrol the deep blue, while white tips rest on sandy patches. Strong currents attract large pelagics—mantas, dolphins, and occasional whale sharks. The rocky outcrop is covered in vibrant corals and sponges. Depth ranges from 15 to 40 meters, best for advanced divers. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. The site offers thrilling drift dives with dramatic drop-offs. Cocos Island’s remote location ensures pristine conditions.
